questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is held up as the supreme ideal of the pativrata, the devoted wife?
questions.explanation: Sita's unwavering faithfulness to Rama makes her the epic's model of the pativrata.

questions.q: What does the term 'pativrata' essentially describe?
options: A wife devoted and faithful to her husband
questions.explanation: A pativrata is a woman whose vow and devotion are centered on her husband.

questions.q: To which husband is Sita faithfully devoted throughout the epic?
questions.explanation: Sita is the loyal wife of Rama, prince of Ayodhya.

questions.q: When Rama was exiled to the forest, what did Sita choose to do?
questions.explanation: Sita insisted on following Rama into the forest rather than remain in comfort without him.

questions.q: Who abducted Sita and held her captive, testing her fidelity?
questions.explanation: The demon king Ravana carried Sita off to Lanka, but she remained faithful to Rama.

questions.q: In which garden was the captive Sita kept while she resisted Ravana?
questions.explanation: Sita was confined in the Ashoka Vatika in Lanka, where she kept her thoughts fixed on Rama.

questions.q: How did Sita respond to Ravana's repeated demands that she become his queen?
options: She asked for time to decide
questions.explanation: Sita rejected Ravana entirely, keeping her devotion to Rama unbroken.

questions.q: What ordeal did Sita undergo to prove her purity after her rescue?
questions.explanation: Sita entered fire in the Agni Pariksha to affirm her unstained virtue.

questions.q: Which god bore Sita unharmed out of the flames during her trial by fire?
questions.explanation: Agni, the fire god, carried Sita out unburned, attesting to her chastity.

questions.q: Who is Sita's father, the king who raised this model of wifely virtue?
questions.explanation: Sita is the daughter of King Janaka of Mithila.

questions.q: What did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama as proof she lived and remained faithful?
questions.explanation: Sita entrusted Hanuman with her Chudamani, her hair-jewel, as a token for Rama.

questions.q: While captive, what did Sita place between herself and Ravana when he approached her?
questions.explanation: Sita kept a blade of grass before her, refusing even to look directly at Ravana.

questions.q: Which faithful demoness guarded and consoled Sita in the Ashoka grove?
questions.explanation: The rakshasi Trijata befriended Sita and reported her prophetic dream of Rama's victory.

questions.q: In despair during captivity, what did Sita at one point consider doing?
questions.explanation: Overcome by separation from Rama, Sita contemplated suicide before Hanuman brought hope.

questions.q: When Hanuman offered to carry Sita home on his back, why did she decline?
options: She would not be touched by a man other than Rama, and wished Rama to win her honorably
She did not believe he was real
questions.explanation: Sita refused so that no man but Rama would touch her and so Rama could win her by his own valor.

questions.q: What did the wife of Brahmin Atri, an elder pativrata, gift Sita at her hermitage?
options: Celestial garments, ornaments, and unfading garlands
questions.explanation: Anasuya, the devoted wife of sage Atri, blessed Sita with celestial robes, ornaments, and unwithering garlands.

questions.q: The sage's wife who instructed Sita on the duties of a devoted wife was named:
questions.explanation: Anasuya, Atri's wife and herself a renowned pativrata, taught Sita the dharma of a faithful wife.

questions.q: Which disguise did Ravana use to lure Sita and seize her from the hermitage?
questions.explanation: Ravana came as a begging ascetic to draw Sita past the protective line before abducting her.

questions.q: What protective boundary did Lakshmana draw, which Sita crossed before her abduction?
options: A line on the ground (the Lakshmana rekha in later tradition)
questions.explanation: In popular tradition Sita stepped beyond the protective line Lakshmana drew, exposing her to Ravana.

questions.q: After Sita's first banishment from Ayodhya, in whose hermitage did she take refuge?
questions.explanation: The exiled Sita found shelter in the hermitage of the sage Valmiki, where she raised her sons.

questions.q: What were the names of the twin sons Sita bore and raised in the forest?
questions.explanation: Sita gave birth to and raised Rama's twin sons, Luva (Lava) and Kusha, in Valmiki's ashrama.

questions.q: Even in long separation, on whom did Sita keep her mind constantly fixed as a pativrata?
questions.explanation: The mark of Sita's fidelity was that her thoughts never wavered from Rama.

questions.q: When Hanuman first revealed himself to Sita, what initial fear made her doubt him?
options: That he was Ravana in disguise
That he was a forest spirit
That he was a messenger of the gods testing her
questions.explanation: Sita at first suspected the monkey might be Ravana in another shape, until Hanuman showed Rama's ring.

questions.q: What token did Hanuman show Sita to prove he truly came from Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman presented Rama's signet ring, which convinced Sita that he was a genuine envoy.

questions.q: Which curse on Ravana, recalled in the epic, kept him from forcing himself upon the faithful Sita?
options: A curse of the sage Vishvamitra
Brahma's decree that he could not touch an unwilling woman, on pain of his head bursting (linked to Nalakubara's curse)
A curse of Indra binding his strength
A vow he swore to Mandodari
questions.explanation: Ravana was cursed (notably by Nalakubara) that he would die if he took an unwilling woman, sparing Sita force.

questions.q: After the war, when Rama spoke coldly and asked her to prove her purity, how did Sita respond before entering the fire?
options: She calmly affirmed her chastity and called on Agni as witness
She refused and returned to Janaka
questions.explanation: Sita declared her fidelity and invoked Agni as witness before stepping into the flames.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, why was Sita finally banished from Ayodhya despite her proven fidelity?
options: Public rumor and gossip questioned her purity after captivity
She wished to return to the forest
questions.explanation: Rumors among the citizens about her time in Lanka led Rama to send the pregnant Sita away.

questions.q: At the close of her story, how did Sita make her final affirmation of fidelity and depart?
options: She ascended to heaven in a chariot of fire
She vanished into the sacred fire again
She called on the earth, her mother, to receive her, and the ground opened to take her
questions.explanation: Sita invoked the earth goddess to take her as proof of her purity, and the earth opened to receive her.

questions.q: From whom was Sita born, a fact tied to her appeal to be received at her life's end?
options: From a lotus in a pond
From a furrow in the earth, found by Janaka while plowing
From the flames of a sacrifice
questions.explanation: Sita was found by Janaka in a furrow as he plowed, making her a daughter of the earth who returns to it.

questions.q: Which other woman in the Ramayana, faithful to her own husband, prophesied or counseled in a way that honored the ideal of the pativrata?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, Ravana's devoted wife, is herself remembered as a virtuous pativrata who counseled her husband rightly.
